seo.title: Browser Extensions implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Browser Extensions projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist ensures your browser extension meets Manifest V3 standards, security protocols for the Chrome Web Store, and performance requirements for production environments.

items.title: Validate Manifest V3 Compliance
items.description: Ensure manifest_version is set to 3 and no deprecated Manifest V2 keys (like background.scripts or web_accessible_resources as strings) are present.

items.title: Audit Host Permissions
items.description: Verify that host_permissions only include specific domains required for functionality rather than using broad <all_urls> patterns.

items.title: Define Web Accessible Resources
items.description: Explicitly list only the specific files (images, fonts, scripts) that need to be accessible to web pages in the web_accessible_resources array with strict matches.

items.title: Check Permission Justification
items.description: Cross-reference the 'permissions' array with your code to remove any unused APIs like 'tabs', 'storage', or 'management' that trigger additional review scrutiny.

items.title: Verify Action Configuration
items.description: Ensure the 'action' key is used instead of 'browser_action' or 'page_action' and includes default_icon, default_title, and default_popup.

items.title: Remove Global Variable Dependencies
items.description: Verify that the background service worker does not rely on global state; use chrome.storage.local or session to persist data across worker restarts.

items.title: Implement Event Listeners at Top Level
items.description: Ensure all chrome.runtime.onMessage and other event listeners are registered synchronously at the top level of the service worker script.

items.title: Replace Timers with Alarms API
items.description: Convert all instances of setInterval or setTimeout in background scripts to the chrome.alarms API to ensure execution after service worker suspension.

items.title: Handle Message Port Disconnects
items.description: Add try-catch blocks and check for chrome.runtime.lastError when using sendResponse to prevent service worker crashes on port closure.

items.title: Optimize Service Worker Startup
items.description: Measure service worker boot time; ensure it completes execution in under 50ms to prevent browser-side termination during heavy load.

items.title: Enforce Content Security Policy (CSP)
items.description: Define a strict CSP in manifest.json that disallows 'unsafe-eval' and restricts script-src to 'self'.

items.title: Sanitize DOM Injections
items.description: Use DOMPurify or a similar library when injecting dynamic content into the page via content scripts to prevent XSS vulnerabilities.

items.title: Externalize AI API Keys
items.description: Verify that no LLM or third-party API keys are hardcoded in the extension; use a proxy backend or chrome.storage for user-provided keys.

items.title: Validate External Messaging
items.description: Check that chrome.runtime.onMessageExternal is either not used or has a strict whitelist of allowed sender IDs to prevent unauthorized control.

items.title: Audit Storage Sensitivity
items.description: Ensure PII or authentication tokens are stored in chrome.storage.local (unencrypted) only if necessary, or use a secure remote session.

items.title: Implement Shadow DOM for UI
items.description: Wrap all injected UI elements in a Shadow Root to prevent host page CSS from leaking into the extension and vice versa.

items.title: Verify CSS Prefixing
items.description: If not using Shadow DOM, ensure all CSS classes use a unique, extension-specific prefix to avoid naming collisions with target websites.

items.title: Limit Content Script Execution
items.description: Use 'matches' and 'exclude_matches' in the manifest to restrict content scripts to only the specific sub-paths where functionality is required.

items.title: Check Main World Leakage
items.description: Ensure content scripts do not expose sensitive extension functions to the window object of the host page.

items.title: Test Dynamic Injection
items.description: If using chrome.scripting.executeScript, verify that the 'target' object includes the correct tabId and frameIds.

items.title: Asset Size Optimization
items.description: Verify that the total extension package size is minimized; compress all PNG/SVG icons and minify bundled JavaScript.

items.title: Verify Icon Sets
items.description: Confirm that icons are provided in all required sizes: 16x16, 32x32, 48x48, and 128x128 pixels.

items.title: Check Storage Sync Limits
items.description: If using chrome.storage.sync, verify that the data payload per item is under 8KB and the total is under 100KB.

items.title: Implement Loading States
items.description: Ensure the popup or side panel displays a loading indicator immediately if data is being fetched from an external API or background script.

items.title: Validate Offline Behavior
items.description: Test extension behavior when the browser is offline; ensure UI elements fail gracefully without hanging.

items.title: Privacy Policy URL Validation
items.description: Ensure the privacy policy URL is active and specifically describes how user data is collected, used, and shared per CWS Developer Terms.

items.title: Single Purpose Verification
items.description: Review the extension description and features to ensure it serves a single, clear purpose as required by Chrome Web Store policies.

items.title: Prepare Promotional Tiles
items.description: Verify that the 440x280 small tile, 920x680 large tile, and 1400x560 marquee tile images are ready and meet Chrome's design guidelines.

items.title: Automate Versioning
items.description: Integrate a build script that syncs the version number in package.json with the version field in manifest.json before every release.

items.title: Cross-Browser Compatibility Test
items.description: Run the extension in Firefox (using polyfills if necessary) and Safari to ensure logic doesn't rely on Chrome-only non-standard behaviors.
